items.title: Malaysia targets higher local vehicle content, warns against import dependence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is pushing for higher local content in vehicle manufacturing as the government seeks to strengthen domestic suppliers and reduce the growing dependence on imported vehicles and components. Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said investors must play a bigger role in developing Malaysia's industrial ecosystem rather than simply selling vehicles. They must be committed to building the industry in Malaysia, involving local SMEs and helping to develop the nation's supply chain."

items.title: Indonesia, India announce BrahMos missile deal
items.summary: JAKARTA: India will supply Indonesia with long-range missiles, an Indian official said on Tuesday as their leaders agreed to deepen ties in defence, critical minerals and other areas. An agreement for "cooperation on BrahMos System" was struck during the visit, Indian foreign ministry spokesman Randhir Jaiswal said in a post on X. The two countries also reached an agreement on air-to-air missile cooperation that "enhances technology collaboration and defence capabilities", he said.

items.title: Muhyiddin did not interfere in Jana Wibawa process, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: Former finance minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz said this while testifying as key prosecution witness in Muhyiddin's trial involving seven charges - four counts of abuse of power and three counts of money laundering linked to the programme. Under cross-examination by Muhyiddin's lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, the witness also agreed that the accused never interfered in an evaluation process conducted by the Jana Wibawa programme Contractor Evaluation Committee. Tengku Zafrul: Based on my knowledge, no.

items.title: My client is autistic, says lawyer of Banting school stabbing suspect
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 15-year-old suspect in the stabbing of a female student at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, is autistic, her lawyer said. That is all I can say," she said after the remand proceedings at the Telok Datok Magistrate's Court today. Kuala Langat police chief, Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi, said the remand period would allow police to gather further evidence and record statements from those involved.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 7, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's benchmark index ended lower on Tuesday as investors took mild profits following the recent rebound in the local market. Meanwhile, cryptocurrencies trended lower, with Bitcoin easing to RM257,063, while Ethereum slipped to RM7,216. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Anwar says Putrajaya mulling Land Act amendment for multiple houses on Felda lots
items.summary: MARAN, July 7 — The government plans to amend the Land (Group Settlement Areas) Act 1960 (Act 530) to allow more than one house to be built on a single residential lot owned by Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) settlers, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said he had instructed Felda to prepare draft amendments to the Act within two months for submission to the Cabinet before they are tabled in Parliament later this year. Meanwhile, Anwar said the Felda New Generation Housing Project (PGBF), introduced in 2013, covers 43 sites involving a total of 8,224 housing units across seven states, namely Pahang, Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan and Perak.

items.title: PM: Petronas dividends make up only 6 pct of govt's 2026 revenue
items.summary: He said the national oil company's contribution accounts for only about five to six per cent of the government's total projected revenue this year. In a written parliamentary reply, Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the government's projected revenue for 2026 stands at RM343.1 billion, comprising RM270.4 billion in tax revenue and RM72.7 billion in non-tax revenue. "The federal government's main source of revenue is tax revenue, which accounts for more than 70 per cent of total government revenue, while Petronas dividends represent only around five to six per cent of the total estimated federal government revenue for 2026.

items.title: Malaysia schools need proactive safety policy, not reactive responses, says official
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government has been urged to draw up a National School Safety Master Plan as a comprehensive policy to strengthen the protection of students following several incidents of violence in schools that have sparked public concern in recent times. Federal Territories State Leadership Council Education Bureau chairman Zaleha Dullah said the master plan should encompass physical security, risk management, emergency procedures and a standardised monitoring mechanism for implementation across all schools. She said the plan could be formulated through a National School Safety Roundtable involving the Ministry of Education (MOE), security agencies, psychologists, academics, parents’ associations, civil society organisations and student representatives.

items.title: Banting stabbing: Suhakam urges public not to speculate on child suspect
items.summary: Suhakam's Office of the Children's Commissioner (OCC) said speculation about the circumstances of the case, including the child's medical condition, disability, mental health or alleged motive, was irresponsible. Suhakam said media reports indicated the suspect's legal counsel had stated that the child was autistic. While the case is under police investigation, Suhakam said the rights, dignity and best interests of every child involved must be respected throughout the legal process.

items.title: Reservoir Link's unit secures sand control equipment supply and services contract from Petronas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Reservoir Link Energy Bhd's unit, Reservoir Link Sdn Bhd, has secured a five-year contract from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd to provide remedial sand control equipment and services. The contract will run until May 20, 2031. The contract covers the supply of sand screens, accessories and related services for wire wrap and wire mesh screen systems, as well as sand consolidation and sand agglomeration chemicals.

items.title: New mosques, surau must include facilities for PwDs, senior citizens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: All new mosque and surau developments nationwide must incorporate facilities and infrastructure for persons with disabilities (PwDs) and senior citizens to ensure places of worship are more inclusive and accessible. She said most mosques and surau registered under the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) already provide basic facilities for senior citizens and PwDs. However, she said the government was prioritising efforts to upgrade these facilities through existing funding mechanisms, including maintenance allocations, mosque funds, community contributions and financial assistance coordinated by the respective state religious authorities.

items.title: Three terrace houses, van damaged and resident suffers burns in Penang fire
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said the department received an emergency call at 2.43pm. The first fire engine from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 2.54pm after encountering traffic congestion en route. He said the fire involved three permanent single-storey terrace houses, each measuring about 20ft by 70ft.

items.title: Petros CEO, CFO received confidential Petronas document, court told
items.summary: The disclosure surfaced during the trial of former Petronas manager Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, who is accused of attempting to disclose a confidential company document to Petros. Qurratu Aini, the prosecution's third witness, also said that Khairul Akmal had applied for a senior position at Petros several months before the alleged disclosure. She said Khairul Akmal applied for the position of Head Upstream Finance through Petros' online recruitment system on Jan 24, 2024.

items.title: Semporna villagers remove 827kg of waste from village, waters
items.summary: SEMPORNA: The villagers here have removed 827.3kg of waste from their village and surrounding waters through a recent community-led clean-up. The effort was part of a pilot community-based waste management system implemented by Reef Check Malaysia with the Kampung Sri Aman Village Committee. The team also worked with waste management company AMWIL, which deployed boats to collect floating debris from surrounding waters, removing another 350.8kg of waste comprising discarded cloth, rubber mats and other bulky materials.

items.title: Johor election: KTMB ramps up ETS services with 20pc discount
items.summary: Following a total sell-out of an earlier batch of tickets on June 19, KTMB is deploying eight additional services across two key routes: KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral and JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral. For those planning their trip, ticket sales for the JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral route opened today at 3:00 pm. Tickets for the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route will go on sale Wednesday at 9:00 am.

items.title: RM5,000 to wed across the border: Why some Malaysians head to Thailand
items.summary: The trend was revealed by veteran Thai imam Haji Yusof, 74, who has solemnised marriages involving Malaysian couples for more than two decades. He said the agents, mostly Thai nationals, arranged the entire cross-border marriage process, from transporting couples into southern Thailand to securing the required religious documents. Yusof said Malaysian couples rarely chose mosques in Tak Bai or Sungai Golok, instead preferring agents who could expedite the process.

items.title: RM980mil debt burden: Anwar warns Felda against past errors
items.summary: To repay debt. Whose debt? Felda's debt.

items.title: Corporate espionage case: Ex-Petronas manager sent upstream performance report to Petros email, witness says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The Sessions Court was told today that Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, a former Petronas manager, had applied to work at Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) months before he allegedly leaked the national oil company’s financial and business secrets to the same company. Petros Human Resource Analyst Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila Junaidi, 40, testified that Mohd Khairul Akmal had submitted an application for the Head Upstream Finance position through its website’s internal system on Jan 24, 2024. “On March 27, 2024, I contacted Mohd Khairul Akmal via email and telephone to extend a job offer for the position of Head Finance (Gas Aggregator & Sarawak Resource Management (SRM)), with a monthly salary of RM25,000.

items.title: Johor election: Early voting concludes at all 62 centres
items.summary: JOHOR BARU:The early voting process for the 16th Johor State Election involving security personnel and their spouses concluded today after all 62 early voting centres closed by 5pm. Earlier, EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun told a press conference at Dewan Mahkota, Kem Mahkota in Kluang that 29 early voting centres were closed at noon. Another five early voting centres closed at 2pm, while the remaining 28 closed at 5pm.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends marginally lower on mild profit-taking
items.summary: Turnover edged up to 2.69 billion units valued at RM2.06 billion from 2.68 billion units valued at RM1.69 billion on Monday. Warrants turnover dropped to 940.84 billion units worth RM136.91 million versus 1.04 billion units worth RM141.68 million previously. The ACE Market volume declined to 552.24 million units valued at RM156.24 million from 571.10 million units valued at RM193.68 million yesterday.

items.title: Loke: Policy decisions made via consensus, not dictated by DAP
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Government decisions at both the federal and state levels are made collectively through consensus among all component parties rather than dictated by any single faction. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke dismissed claims that the administration operates under the thumb of DAP, labelling it an outdated narrative designed to undermine Pakatan Harapan (PH). "In the federal government, all parties, including DAP, Umno, and PKR, present their views before decisions are finalised by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim," he told reporters here today.

items.title: Drug syndicate crippled after Perlis raids uncover RM34.31 million haul
items.summary: PADANG BESAR: Police have crippled a drug trafficking syndicate in Perlis following the arrest of three people and the seizure of liquid drugs worth RM34.31 million. Perlis police chief Datuk Muhammad Abdul Halim said the suspects, comprising a Thai woman and two local men aged between 38 and 68, were arrested in three separate raids across the state between July 1 and July 3. Muhammad said police seized 35 boxes containing 21,000 liquid drug cartridges weighing 39.9 litres, valued at RM5.9 million, during the second raid.

items.title: Malaysia revamps auto incentives, prioritises high-value investments and strengthen SMEs [WATCH]
items.summary: Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said the enhanced incentive framework forms part of the government's broader strategy to position Malaysia as a manufacturing and export gateway to the Asean market of more than 700 million people. Johari said Malaysia's automotive strategy extends beyond vehicle production and focuses on building a resilient manufacturing ecosystem. Johari said Malaysia's domestic market of about 34 million people should be viewed alongside its strategic position within Asean.

items.title: ‘Preparations made’: Nadma says govt agencies ready for possible ‘Super El Nino’
items.summary: PUCHONG, July 7 — Government agencies responsible for disaster management are on high alert ahead of the possible onset of a ‘Super El Nino’ towards the end of the year, while also stepping up preparations to mitigate the risks of extreme heat, haze and open burning during the Southwest Monsoon. National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said the coordination meeting held today was not because agencies were unprepared, but to update the public on their current state of readiness and strengthen coordination among the ministries and agencies involved. “All agencies have taken the necessary measures.

items.title: Govt channels over RM12mil to empower Indian community
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The government through the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has allocated more than RM12 million to empower the Indian community through the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme (Celik Madani) 2026 and the Third Series of Dharma Madani Programme. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said that of the total, RM8.87 million was approved under the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme Celik Madani 2026 involving 162 kindergartens nationwide and benefiting 3,612 Indian children from B40 families. Meanwhile, RM3.36 million was approved for the Third Series of Dharma Madani Programme to 168 Hindu houses of worship nationwide.

items.title: Padi, rice law revamp: Fines to rise up to RM500,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proposed amendments to the Control of Padi and Rice Act 1994 will significantly increase penalties for offences while expanding regulatory powers over rice grading, pricing and the movement of padi and rice. The Control of Padi and Rice (Amendment) Bill 2026 was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. The Bill also seeks to amend Section 29 to empower the minister to regulate the licensing of padi buyers, determine rice grades and impose price controls on graded rice.

items.title: Rohingya man caught driving without licence in RTD operation
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A 32-year-old Rohingya man was arrested after he was caught driving a multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) without a valid licence during a Kedah Road Transport Department (RTD) enforcement operation last night. Kedah RTD director Stien Van Lutam said the suspect, who works as a construction worker and also operates a grocery store, was detained at about 11pm during Ops PeWa, following public complaints and intelligence gathered by the department. Stien said the suspect told investigators he was on his way home after closing his grocery store when he was stopped by enforcement officers.

items.title: Sharing Banting stabbing children’s identities could land you in jail, warns Suhakam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia’s human rights commission has warned that sharing photographs, videos or other identifying information about the children involved in the recent Banting school stabbing could be a criminal offence. In a statement today, the Office of the Children’s Commissioner of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) urged the public to stop circulating content that could harm both the victim and the alleged attacker. It said both children were entitled to legal protections under the Child Act 2001 and the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child, regardless of the ongoing police investigation.

items.title: Zahid pays tribute to former journalist and Umno media officer Jailani Harun
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has expressed his condolences to the family of former journalist and media officer at the Umno President’s Office Datuk Jailani Harun, who died today. “I extend my heartfelt condolences to the entire family of the late Jailani. Jailani died at 11.34am at Serdang Hospital.

items.title: Post mortem shows Durian Tunggal victims were executed, say lawyers
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The families of the three men killed in a police operation in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, last November have alleged that post-mortem reports contradicted the police's claim that the officers acted in self-defence. Lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan who is representing the families said the post-mortem reports have made it clear that this was an execution-style killing. He said the reports revealed downward bullet trajectories and evidence of close-range gunfire, which he said were inconsistent with the official account that the victims were shot after allegedly attacking the police personnel.

items.title: Health Ministry records 73 heat-related illness cases, four deaths since Jan 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 73 heat-related illness cases have been recorded nationwide since Jan 1, according to the Health Ministry's Disease Control Division. Public Health Medicine Specialist Dr Hazlina Yahaya said the cases comprised 59 instances of heat exhaustion, six of exertional heat stroke, four of classical heat stroke, two of postpartum hyperpyrexia, and two of heat cramps. "The cumulative death toll remains at four cases, involving two adult males aged 42 in Penang and 22 in Kedah, as well as two children aged two in Negri Sembilan and four in Perak," she said.

items.title: Indonesia's 2026 budget deficit seen widening to 2.85pct of GDP
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's budget deficit is expected to widen to 2.85 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP) this year, bigger than originally predicted, the government said on Tuesday, even as it aims to scale back spending on the president's flagship school meals programme. It is also wider than both the government's previous estimate of a deficit of 2.68 per cent this year and the 2025 budget gap of 2.81 per cent of GDP. For example, in the first half of last year the budget deficit was 0.84 per cent of GDP.

items.title: Police call for stronger school, family support after Banting stabbing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have called for closer coordination between schools, families and mental health professionals to strengthen intervention efforts and reduce the risk of violent incidents involving students. Federal police Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said while such incidents could not be completely prevented, early identification, consistent treatment and stronger support systems could help reduce risks. He said prevention required more than addressing a student's mental health or behavioural issues, with timely communication between parents, schools and medical professionals crucial for effective intervention.

items.title: SkyeChip advances interface IP engagement with Cerebras
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: SkyeChip Bhd is engaging a global artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure company Cerebras Systems Inc in advancing ultra-fast AI computing through its industry-leading wafer-scale engine technology. As part of the engagement, the integrated circuit company is developing custom high-speed interface intellectual property (IP) for US-based Cerebras' next-generation computing initiatives. SkyeChip said the engagement involves milestone-based development, rigorous technical validation and close collaboration across the semiconductor ecosystem.

items.title: Sabah looks to develop local talent for semiconductor industry
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government is considering training more local talent to support the semiconductor industry at the new Kota Belud Industrial Park. Sabah Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport permanent secretary Datuk Thomas Logijin said the semiconductor ecosystem generates significant value-added activities and has the potential to substantially increase Sabah's gross domestic product (GDP). At present, Sabah has four established industrial parks: Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park (KKIP), Sabah Agro-Industrial Precinct, Sandakan Industrial Park, and Sipitang Oil and Gas Industrial Park.

items.title: Anwar unveils seven incentives for Felda settlers, including new generation housing
items.summary: MARAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced seven incentives for Felda settlers covering housing, education, and healthcare initiatives. Speaking at the national-level 2026 Felda Settlers' Day celebration and Felda's 70th anniversary, Anwar said the government will establish a dedicated committee to resolve long-standing issues involving second-generation Felda settlers. "The government fully understands the concerns of the new generation of Felda settlers regarding housing, particularly issues related to the concept of building two houses on a single lot (2R1L).

items.title: Maybank Islamic launches 14pct fixed-rate Syariah-compliant credit card
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Islamic Bhd has introduced a Syariah-compliant credit card offering a fixed profit rate of 14 per cent per annum, as the bank looks to provide a simpler and more predictable financing option for consumers. The Nadi Mastercard Credit Card-i carries no annual fee and does not impose compounding charges; it is aimed at customers seeking straightforward access to credit with greater certainty over financing costs. "Nadi Mastercard Credit Card-i reflects this shift by making access to credit more straightforward, predictable and accessible," it said in a statement.

items.title: Incoming: Super El Niño could bring heat and haze from November, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia could face hotter weather, lower rainfall and a greater risk of transboundary haze from late this year as MetMalaysia forecasts a Super El Niño to develop in November and last until January 2027. Ambun said El Niño conditions were already present, but Super El Niño status was only expected to begin around November. If realised, the phenomenon could bring significantly lower rainfall and higher temperatures to Malaysia early next year.

items.title: Teacher, accomplice jailed eight years for trafficking woman for sexual exploitation
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 7 — The Sessions Court here today found a teacher and his accomplice guilty of trafficking a woman for sexual exploitation and sentenced them to eight years’ imprisonment and RM5,000 fine each. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli handed down the sentence on Mohd Hasfyanizam Mansor, 50, and labourer Mohamad Fazley Jaafar, 31, at the end of the defence case. Deputy public prosecutors Ahmad Hafizudin Manjur Ahmad and Noradila Abdul Latif prosecuted, while lawyer Mohd Shahir Mat Jusoh represented the two accused.

items.title: Three men plead not guilty to setting fire to activist Cikgu Chandra’s wife’s cars
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three men pleaded not guilty today in the Petaling Jaya Sessions Court to charges of setting fire to three cars belonging to the wife of activist ‘Cikgu Chandra’ in March. The accused, tow-truck driver A. Kumaran, 34; security guard S. Kisshen Nair, 32; and lorry driver P. Surendran, 37, are jointly charged with committing mischief by fire on two Perodua vehicles and a Proton automobile owned by M. Sri Uma Mahesvary, 50. Judge Noor Ruzilawati Mohd Nor allowed RM8,000 in bail for each accused, subject to the additional conditions sought by the prosecution.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain forecast in nine states, KL and Putrajaya until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds have been forecasted in nine states, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya till 6pm. The Malaysian Meteorological Department issued the warning for Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim), Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Bentong, Temerloh, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), and Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat and Sepang). Over in Sarawak, the warning includes Kuching, Sarikei (Julau), Sibu (Kanowit), Mukah (Mukah), Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu dan Sebauh), Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: MACC, Armed Forces tighten information-sharing in fight against corruption
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening strategic cooperation, particularly in intelligence sharing, information exchange and governance enhancement, to bolster anti-corruption efforts and safeguard national interests. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission highly values its longstanding cooperation with the Armed Forces and expressed hope that the synergy between the two agencies will continue to grow stronger. Also present were MACC Intelligence Division senior director Datuk Saiful Ezral Arifin and MAF Security and Counter Intelligence Directorate director Lt Col Muhamad Zainol Md Yusof.

items.title: EC says Johor’s 56-seat election could be decided by 10pm on Saturday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July7 — Voters are expected to know the outcome of all 56 seats in Saturday’s Johor state election by about 10pm, after the Election Commission of Malaysia upgraded its election results management system. According to Buletin TV3 , Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the improvements should enable the commission to announce the full outcome by around 10pm. Buletin TV3 said Ramlan also urged eligible voters to turn out in large numbers on Saturday to fulfil their democratic responsibility.

items.title: New mosques, surau must include facilities for PWDs and seniors, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — All new mosque and surau developments in the country must incorporate facilities and infrastructure that cater to persons with disabilities (PWDs) and senior citizens to ensure places of worship are more inclusive and accessible, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. She said that, to date, the majority of mosques and surau registered under the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (JAWI) have already provided basic facilities for senior citizens and PWDs. However, she said the government is now prioritising efforts to upgrade these facilities through existing funding mechanisms, including maintenance allocations, mosque funds, community contributions and financial assistance coordinated by the respective state religious authorities within their jurisdictions.

items.title: EC aims for 96pc turnout in early voting
items.summary: KLUANG, July 7 — The Election Commission (EC) is targeting a 96 per cent turnout for early voting in the Johor state election today. “Based on our previous experience, early voting turnout can reach as high as 96 per cent, and we are maintaining that projection,” he told a press conference after observing the early voting process at Kem Mahkota here. Commenting on today’s early voting process, Ramlan said 62 early voting centres were opened across the state at 8am.

items.title: In Larkin, land leases and a city’s growing pains shape Johor election contest
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Land lease renewals in Kampung Melayu Majidee and the need to modernise public infrastructure have emerged as the key issues shaping the contest for the Larkin state seat in the 16th Johor state election. Mohd Hairi, who is also State Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman, said the state government remained committed to safeguarding Kampung Melayu Majidee through lease renewal offers of between 60 and 99 years, either on an individual or lot basis, as well as a 50 per cent premium discount to ease the financial burden on residents. Suhaizan, who is Pulai MP, however, said the state government’s measures had yet to meet residents’ expectations.

items.title: MACC: Three arrested in Kedah over alleged RM20m financing bid for paddy and rice purchases that never happened
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three people, including two company directors, have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in Kedah over allegations they used false documents in an attempt to obtain about RM20 million in financing for paddy and rice purchases that investigators say never took place. In a statement today, the anti-graft agency said the suspects, who are involved in the rice and paddy production and processing business, are suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars to a development financial institution under the Trade Working Capital Financing-I facility. The financing, worth about RM20 million, was intended for the purchase of paddy and rice.

items.title: ‘The tears just won’t stop’: Father says Banting stabbing victim leaves ICU but remains traumatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The 15-year-old girl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting remains deeply traumatised despite her condition stabilising after surgery, her father said today. The tears just won’t stop flowing,” he said. Mohd Firuz said doctors and nurses were continuing to monitor the teenager’s condition.

items.title: Perak Ruler officiates Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: IPOH, July 7 — The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, today officially opened the Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta here. The Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, the Raja Muda of Perak, Raja Jaafar Raja Muda Musa, and the Raja DiHilir of Perak, Raja Iskandar Dzurkarnain Sultan Idris Shah also attended the event. In his speech, Saifuddin Nasution said the opening of the camp symbolises the MADANI Government’s commitment to strengthening the capabilities of the nation’s security forces to address increasingly complex and evolving security challenges.

items.title: Election Commission says 588 complaints lodged as Johor polls enter final stretch
items.summary: KLUANG, July 7 — The Election Commission (EC) has received 588 complaints involving various election offences during the campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, as of today. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said that, of the total, 44 police reports had been lodged. Earlier, Ramlan, accompanied by Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman, monitored the smooth conduct of the polling process at several early voting centres, including the Kluang District Police Headquarters and Kem Mahkota.

items.title: Environment Department denies issuing viral 2024 state cleanliness infographic
items.summary: “The public is advised not to disseminate the information or use it as an official reference without first verifying it with the department. The department said the spread of unverified information could not only mislead the public but also potentially erode public confidence in official information relating to environmental management and conservation. To avoid confusion, the DOE said all official media statements, reports, statistics, infographics, announcements and information issued by the department would only be published through its official communication channels, namely its official portal at www.doe.gov.my, Facebook (@JabatanAlamSekitar), Instagram (@jabatanalamsekitar), X (@jasmalaysia), TikTok (@jas_malaysia_official) and YouTube (@JabatanAlamSekitar).

items.title: Johor candidates pause campaigning to observe early voting by security personnel
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Despite their busy campaign schedule ahead of polling day this Saturday, Johor state election candidates took time to observe the early voting process for security personnel today. All three candidates for the Tenggaroh seat — Muhamad Amerul Muhamad (PN), Mohd Youzaimi Yusof (BN) and Md Yusof Dawam (PH) — also did likewise, at the Iskandar Camp early voting centre in Mersing. Besides candidates, top military and security officers also visited early voting centres, including Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman together with 21st Special Service Group (21 GGK) commander Major General Datuk Ahmad Shuhaimi Mat Wajab at Iskandar Camp, Mersing.

items.title: Govt reviewing indefinite detention of juvenile offenders under Child Act, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government is reviewing Section 97 of the Child Act 2001 (Act 611), including introducing a clearer detention period, to strike a balance between justice for victims, public safety and the rehabilitation of child offenders. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the review is being undertaken by a special committee formed following the abolition of the mandatory death penalty and life imprisonment in 2023. Kulasegaran said one of the key reasons for reviewing the provision was the situation faced by individuals who were detained as children without a clearly defined detention period.

items.title: Father of stabbed Banting schoolgirl says he is willing to meet suspect’s family
items.summary: BANTING, July 7 — The father of the 15-year-old girl who was injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting has said he is willing to meet the family of the teenage suspect, adding that he hopes his daughter receives “the justice she deserves”. According to Sinar Harian , Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he had not received any contact from the suspect’s family since yesterday’s attack. Mohd Firuz said he hoped appropriate action would be taken against those responsible and that the incident would serve as a lesson, given that it occurred on school grounds.

items.title: ‘My daughter is not a bully,’ says father of girl stabbed at Banting school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The father of the 15-year-old girl stabbed at a secondary school in Banting has denied social media allegations that his daughter was involved in bullying, describing her as a reserved teenager and urging the public to leave the investigation to police. According to Sinar Harian , Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he had seen WhatsApp messages and a viral list of names linking his daughter to alleged bullying, but stressed that neither he nor his family could verify their authenticity. Describing his daughter’s personality, Mohd Firuz said she rarely went anywhere without one of her parents.

items.title: DOSM: Malaysia’s health inflation doubles to 3pc in 2025 on higher insurance and service costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — Health inflation in Malaysia increased to three per cent in 2025 from 1.4 per cent the previous year, driven by rising healthcare service costs. Meanwhile, inflation for medicines rose to 2.7 per cent from 2.2 per cent in 2024, while health equipment increased to 1.2 per cent from 0.6 per cent. He said health inflation rates in selected countries in 2025 ranged between negative 0.8 per cent and 5.3 per cent.

items.title: Economy minister: Govt prioritises reducing aid exclusion errors as Malaysia prepares for ageing society
items.summary: He said preparations for an ageing nation required continuous economic and fiscal policies, as well as coordination of five key pillars under the National Ageing Action Plan (NAB) to safeguard the people’s well-being. “...we are developing policies to manage the challenges of an ageing population because Malaysia has already become an ageing nation. Replying to a question from Mohd Nazri Abu Hassan (PN-Merbok) on Malaysia’s preparedness to face an ageing society by 2040, including elderly welfare, healthcare, social protection and family support, Akmal Nasrullah said the country attained ageing nation status in 2021.

items.title: MoE weighs centralising Malaysia’s preschool management as it plans 1,040 new classes by 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — In a major expansion of early childhood education, the Ministry of Education (MOE) plans to roll out 1,040 new preschool classes by 2027, marking a key milestone for the Madani government’s five-year tenure. According to the MOE, the initiative, launched in 2023, reflects the government’s commitment to expanding early childhood education and ensuring no child misses out on quality learning, regardless of their location or background. As of May 31, the ministry is operating 10,491 preschool classes nationwide, benefiting 217,026 students.

items.title: Malaysia’s holiday homes generated RM2.2b in 2025, first official DOSM data shows
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — A total of 71,101 holiday homes were operating nationwide in 2025, generating an estimated RM2.2 billion in revenue, according to the Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. “The holiday homes industry continues to create positive economic spillover effects through higher demand for accommodation, transportation, food and beverage services, retail trade and recreational activities. The Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 is the first official statistical publication on holiday homes by state and district in Malaysia.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar says Sunday voting remarks were personal view, not pressure on Election Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that his remarks regarding the Johor state election dates were merely a personal view aimed at encouraging a higher voter turnout, and not an attempt to interfere in the Election Commission’s (EC) decision. He said the EC was an independent body with the sole authority to determine polling dates, although he believed Sunday voting would make it easier for Malaysians working in Singapore to return home to cast their ballots. “Many Malaysians are working in Singapore, and many of them work half-day on Saturdays.
